ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for spicy chicken nuggets; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared fast-food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 6-piece serving of spicy chicken nuggets weighs approximately 100 g and is typically consumed as a snack or light meal component.

nutrient_source.title: 6Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Spicy — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '6Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Spicy' (cr_id G00010020). All core macros and available micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for breaded fried chicken nuggets (FDC ID 171477) as HPB does not report these fields for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — breaded fried chicken / chicken nuggets analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific spicy chicken nugget product. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for breaded fried chicken products (medium GI range 60–70), consistent with the refined wheat-flour breading and deep-frying preparation. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 14.3) / 100 = 9.4 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product; a single 100 g serving delivers 708 mg sodium, representing approximately 35% of the 2000 mg daily limit, and pairing with dipping sauces will further increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-flour breading with medium GI (66) raises postprandial blood glucose; high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of these nuggets; the refined breading elevates postprandial glucose, the high fat and trans fat content worsen insulin resistance, and spicy sauces may contribute additional hidden sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.6 g/100 g), saturated fat (~5.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), cholesterol (50 mg/100 g), and very high sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this product; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and high sodium significantly raises LDL cholesterol and blood pressure, both established cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); chilli is a nightshade that may exacerbate inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals; trans fat is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product; deep-frying substantially increases AGE load, trans fat at 0.3 g/100 g is pro-inflammatory, and chilli as a nightshade vegetable may worsen joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Nuggets (6-Piece) is a deep-fried, breaded chicken product sold as a fast-food snack or meal component in Singapore, listed in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010020) under the Meat and Alternatives food group. Each nugget consists of minced or formed chicken meat coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil, then finished with a spicy chilli-based seasoning blend. The texture is crispy on the outside and moist inside, with a pronounced spicy heat and savoury flavour. Per 100 g, the product delivers 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g total fat, 14.3 g carbohydrate, and 708 mg sodium. The high sodium content (708 mg/100 g) exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold relevant to hypertension management. The refined breading carries a medium glycemic index (~66), raising postprandial blood glucose, which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes. The estimated trans fat content of 0.3 g/100 g and saturated fat of approximately 5.8 g/100 g are significant cardiovascular risk factors. The product is labelled halal and is suitable for Muslim consumers. It is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Common serving contexts include lunch, dinner, and snack occasions in Singapore fast-food settings.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010020, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central breaded fried chicken nuggets (FDC ID 171477) as HPB does not report these fields. Trans fat value (0.3 g/100 g) carried from input health_suitability notes. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous breaded fried chicken category. Overall confidence is high for macros; medium for saturated fat, dietary fibre, and GI due to analogous estimation.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.8 g/100 g) and dietary fibre (0.8 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171477 (chicken nuggets, breaded, frozen, baked) as HPB does not report these fields for this item. 2. Trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) is carried from the input record's health_suitability caution notes; this value should be verified against product packaging. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific spicy chicken nugget product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken products; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 5. The product is listed as halal in the input cultural dietary_tags. 6. FoodEx2 code A0FKP and FoodOn URI FOODON_03302050 are applied as defensible exact matches for breaded fried chicken nuggets; SNOMED CT has no specific code for this prepared fast-food item.
